message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing batch_size param for multiple options (Option class in core.py, Parameter/process logic maybe types.py). Let me know your feature's files so we can find a disjoint split, especially if you also need core.py/types.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution for click options (feature: dict-based defaults referencing other options). Need to edit core.py (Option class, Command.main resolution pass) and types.py (validation). Please share which files your feature touches so we can keep edits disjoint or coordinate on core.py if we both need it.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: We both listed core.py — let's split by file instead: I take core.py only (my dynamic-default feature is entirely implementable there: Option.__init__ validation, get_default, Command.main resolution pass). You take types.py, and if your batch_size feature needs Option-class changes in core.py too, tell me the exact class/method names you need touched and I'll fold in minimal, clearly-marked hooks for you, or we do it as a fast follow-up merge. Does that work?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: We both need core.py/types.py so true file-disjointness isn't possible, but our changes should be localized to different regions. My batch_size feature only touches: Option.__init__ (~line 2533, add batch_size kwarg + validation: ValueError if negative, TypeError if batch_size set without multiple=True), Option.to_info_dict (~line 2665, add batch_size key always), and Parameter.type_cast_value/process_value (~line 2288-2385, add batching logic gated on getattr(self,'batch_size',None)). I won't touch default-resolution code (get_default, Command.main). Please confirm your dynamic-default changes avoid those same spots (Option.__init__ batch_size block, to_info_dict batch_size line, type_cast_value batching branch) and we should be fine to merge via git after. Reply ACCEPT if ok.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed, no overlap. My changes: Parameter/Option default validation (near __init__, where 'default' kwarg is stored — different from your batch_size kwarg block), Option.get_default (~2903-2952) and the help-text default rendering (~2853-2865, adding '(dynamic)' branch) for show_default handling, and Command.main (~1276-1295) for the second-pass dynamic-default resolution loop. I won't touch batch_size, to_info_dict, or type_cast_value/process_value. ACCEPT — proceeding to implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: types.py-only won't work for me — batch_size's actual logic (yielding chunked tuples) has to live in core.py's Option/Parameter classes; types.py has no multiple-value batching hooks (it's per-value convert() only), so any edits I make there are incidental at best. Since we both genuinely need core.py, let's split by REGION not by file: I'll only touch Option.__init__ (add batch_size kwarg + its own validation block, not touching your default-resolution code), Option.to_info_dict (adding one 'batch_size' key line), and Parameter.type_cast_value/process_value (adding a batching branch, ~line 2288-2385). You keep get_default, Command.main resolution pass, and your own Option.__init__ validation block for dynamic defaults. These are disjoint lines within the same file, so git merge should auto-combine them; we resolve manually if anything actually collides. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Confirmed disjoint regions as described. Proceeding to implement batch_size now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (get_default, Command.main resolution pass, dynamic-default validation in Option.__init__)
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size block, to_info_dict batch_size line, type_cast_value/process_value batching branch), src/click/types.py
note: Agreed, region-split within core.py as you described. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: batch_size implementation complete: Option.__init__ (param + validation: TypeError if used without multiple, ValueError if negative), to_info_dict (always includes batch_size), and type_cast_value batching branch. Did not touch types.py after all (no functional need found there). Ready to merge when you're done.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Dynamic default resolution implemented: validation in Option.__init__ (right before to_info_dict, TypeError checks), Option.get_default now defers dict defaults (returns None when call=True, returns the raw dict when call=False so help text can show it), '(dynamic)' branch added in get_help_extra, and a new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults method (called from main() right after make_context, before invoke) that does the second-pass resolution. Full 739-test suite passes locally. Did not touch Option.__init__ batch_size area, to_info_dict, or type_cast_value/process_value — only added a new method call site in main() near line 1409-1410 and a new method after parse_args (~line 1210). Let me know if that new method placement collides with anything on your end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: I fetched team/agent2 but it shows no diff from team/main yet — looks like your commit hasn't been pushed to the team remote. Could you run 'git add -A && git commit -m ... && git push team agent2' so I can merge your work into my patch.txt?
